mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply capacity, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with the three-month change in INDPRO is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market weakness, which reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: After UNRATE trigger events, the proportion of negative 5-day forward returns is not above 55% over a 20-year backtest, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high relative to consumer price inflation, real interest rates are elevated, compressing equity valuations and discounting future cash flows more heavily, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: When the real-rate spread is above the 200bp threshold, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the rank information coefficient (IC) against forward returns is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle overheating, sustained strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals excess demand that provokes tighter monetary policy, depressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 1% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: After the RSAFS trigger, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative on average, or the signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio below zero over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) signals broad economic expansion and improving corporate profitability, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with the contemporaneous quarterly GDP growth rate is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 15-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates an impending downturn in the housing market and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: After the HOUST trigger, the 60-day forward return is negative in less than 55% of cases, or the average excess return over cash is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: When employment growth (PAYEMS) outpaces industrial production growth (INDPRO), it signals declining productivity and rising unit labor costs, compressing profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth minus INDPRO 3-month growth exceeds 1 percentage point
failure_condition: The divergence between these two measures does not predict negative 20-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion acceleration, when GDP growth exceeds 3%, the Federal Reserve is compelled to raise short-term rates, temporarily increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, but the positive earnings momentum dominates over a 60-day horizon, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During quarters with GDP growth above 3%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is not positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive.
